title: aptamer_affinity_v1 — APTAMER sub-axis affinity output row
description: Output-row schema for _python_bridge/module/aptamer_affinity_sim.py. APTAMER is a specialization SUB-AXIS hanging off the core RIBOZYME axis (an aptamer is a NON-catalytic structured oligonucleotide binder; RIBOZYME = catalytic RNA). Each row carries a Nussinov + Turner-style nearest-neighbour secondary-structure fold and a 1:1 Langmuir equilibrium-binding model. A PASS verifies in-silico simulator-consistency ONLY (AGENTS.tape g8/f2) — never a wet-lab, binding-affinity, therapeutic, clinical, or regulatory claim. No field is derived from the n=6 lattice (g2/f_lattice_fit).

sequence.description: Nucleic-acid sequence, sanitized to RNA alphabet (DNA T folded to U).

ligand.description: Bound ligand / target the aptamer recognizes.

fold.description: Secondary-structure fold: Nussinov 1978 base-pair maximization + Turner-style nearest-neighbour stack-sum folding free energy.

dot_bracket.description: Vienna dot-bracket secondary structure; length equals length_nt.

balanced.description: Dot-bracket parentheses balanced and properly nested.

folding_free_energy_kcal_per_mol.description: Turner-style nearest-neighbour stack-sum folding free energy (kcal/mol, illustrative magnitude); <= 0, more negative = more stable. Anchored to nearest-neighbour folding thermodynamics (SantaLucia 1998; Turner & Mathews 2010).

binding.description: 1:1 Langmuir equilibrium-binding model. Kd = koff/kon; fraction bound theta = [L]/(Kd+[L]); theta = 0.5 at [L] = Kd.

kd_nM.description: Dissociation constant in nanomolar (literature-anchored illustrative magnitude).

kd_recovered_M.description: Kd recomputed as koff/kon — must equal kd_M.

kd_identity_holds.description: True iff kd_recovered_M == kd_M within tolerance (Kd = koff/kon).

theta_at_ligand_eq_kd.description: Fraction bound evaluated at [L] = Kd; must equal 0.5.

isotherm.description: Saturation isotherm sampled over a ligand-concentration grid; monotone non-decreasing in ligand_M.

kcat_per_s.description: Catalytic turnover rate. An aptamer is a NON-catalytic structured-oligonucleotide binder — kcat is exactly 0. This is the defining distinction from the parent RIBOZYME axis (catalytic RNA, kcat > 0).

paper_ref.description: Primary-literature citation for the aptamer / its Kd.

in_silico_caveat.description: Honesty caveat — in-silico simulator-consistency only (AGENTS.tape g8/f2).
